findings.description: The ATTENTION section in the PDF is formatted as a single indented block after 'ATTENTION:', while in the HTML each line is a separate bold paragraph without the indented alignment relative to the ATTENTION label.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'ATTENTION:' label with indented block of four lines to the right. HTML: 'ATTENTION:' on its own line, followed by four separate bold lines left-aligned.

findings.description: The header layout with Governor/Secretary on the left and Director/phone on the right is preserved but the horizontal rule separator present in the HTML version is not in the PDF (PDF uses the department name as centered text without a rule).
findings.evidence: HTML has a horizontal rule under the department name; PDF does not have this explicit rule.

findings.description: The SUBJECT line in the PDF has bold formatting for both the label and the content, while the HTML shows 'SUBJECT:' in bold and the rest in regular weight.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'SUBJECT: Services Information System (SIS) ID Number Merge Reports' appears fully bold. HTML: Only 'SUBJECT:' is bold, the rest is normal text.

findings.description: The bullet point content for DHRSYA SYA515 is cut off at the bottom of PDF page 1 and continues on page 2, whereas in the HTML page 2 it appears as a complete block.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 ends mid-paragraph for the first bullet; HTML page 2 shows the complete bullet content.

findings.description: The 'in the same County' text in the PDF has underlining for emphasis, while in the HTML it appears without underline formatting.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2: 'in the same County' is underlined. HTML page 2: same text lacks underline.

findings.description: The HTML uses h2 for 'Division of Social Services' directly under the h1, which is acceptable, but the document has nested <main> elements (a <main> inside another <main>), which is invalid HTML structure. More importantly, the heading hierarchy doesn't accurately reflect the document structure - 'Division of Social Services' is a subtitle, not a separate section heading.
findings.evidence:
HTML has

North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services

followed by

Division of Social Services

. In the PDF, 'Division of Social Services' is part of the letterhead, not a section heading.

findings.description: The HTML contains a hyperlink to the referenced Dear County Director Letter. While the PDF does appear to contain a hyperlink for this reference, the exact URL cannot be fully verified from the PDF rendering. The link text matches the PDF content.
findings.evidence: HTML: Dear County Director Letter No PM-REM-13-2009. PDF shows 'Dear County Director Letter No PM-REM-13-2009' as a hyperlink.

findings.description: The page header 'Services Information System (SIS) ID Number Merge Reports / Page 2' is included inline in the HTML content. This is a page continuation header from the PDF and its inclusion is not an error per se, but it breaks up the bulleted list unnaturally - the first bullet point's text is split before and after this header.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the first bullet point continues from page 1 to page 2. In the HTML, the page header div is inserted between the introductory text and the <ul> list, but the first bullet's complete text is preserved within the list.
